hero.title: 4 Days, Sacred Maharashtra.
hero.titleAccent: Sai Baba, the Godavari and Lord Shani.
hero.intro: A deeper Maharashtra pilgrimage. Sai Baba's Shirdi, the holy city of Nashik on the Godavari with its Jyotirlinga, and the powerful Shani Shingnapur temple. Four days of devotion and sacred geography.
hero.heroImageAlt: Shirdi Sai Baba and Nashik pilgrimage Maharashtra

overview.heading: 4 Days Shirdi, Nashik & Shani Shingnapur: The Full Picture
body: This 4 day pilgrimage brings together three of Maharashtra's most sacred sites. Shirdi, the home of Sai Baba; Nashik, one of the four cities that hosts the great Kumbh Mela, set on the holy Godavari river; and Shani Shingnapur, the famous temple to Lord Shani in a village with no doors.

Arranged privately from Malaysia, the trip is designed for devotees who want a fuller Maharashtra pilgrimage than a Shirdi-only visit. A driver guide handles the routing between the towns and the temple logistics, so you can focus on darshan and prayer. Nashik also includes the Trimbakeshwar Jyotirlinga, one of the twelve most sacred Shiva shrines.

highlights.heading: Why Travellers from Malaysia Love This Trip

items.title: Sai Baba's Shirdi
items.body: The Samadhi Mandir, Dwarkamai and the sacred sites of Shirdi, the spiritual home of Sai Baba, with its moving daily aartis.

items.title: Nashik & Trimbakeshwar
items.body: The holy city on the Godavari, host of the Kumbh Mela, and the nearby Trimbakeshwar Jyotirlinga, one of the twelve most sacred Shiva temples in India.

items.title: Shani Shingnapur
items.body: The famous village temple to Lord Shani, where legend holds nobody locks their doors, trusting in Shani's protection.

days.title: Arrive Shirdi
days.description: Met at the airport and transferred to Shirdi. Evening darshan or aarti.

days.title: Shirdi & Shani Shingnapur
days.description: Shirdi's sacred sites and a drive to Shani Shingnapur.

days.title: Shirdi to Nashik
days.description: Drive to Nashik for the Godavari ghats and Trimbakeshwar.

days.title: Depart
days.description: A final temple visit before your transfer to the airport.

sections.heading: Shirdi, Nashik & Shani Shingnapur Tour from Malaysia
sections.body:
This 4 day pilgrimage is ideal for devotees who want more than a Shirdi-only visit. It adds Nashik, with its Godavari ghats and the Trimbakeshwar Jyotirlinga, and the powerful Shani Shingnapur temple.

Arranged privately, the trip handles the routing between the towns and the temple logistics, so your focus stays on darshan and prayer.

It is a rich Maharashtra pilgrimage, combining three sacred destinations in one well-paced journey.

sections.heading: The Sacred Sites of Maharashtra
sections.body:
Maharashtra holds some of India's most significant pilgrimage sites. Shirdi draws millions for Sai Baba; Nashik is one of four Kumbh Mela cities and home to Trimbakeshwar, a revered Jyotirlinga; and Shani Shingnapur is famous nationwide.

Visiting them together gives a deeper sense of the region's spiritual landscape than any single stop, which is why this combined pilgrimage is so popular.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for India?
items.answer: Yes. Malaysian passport holders apply for an India e-Visa online before departure. The tourist e-Visa covers tourism and short visits and is valid for 30 days from entry. The application is on the official India e-Visa portal, and Dhesu walks you through it when you book. Visa fees are not included in the package price.

items.question: Which airport does this trip use?
items.answer: This is a land-only package: hotels, transfers, tours and guide are included, but international flights are not. Most Malaysian travellers fly from Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) to Shirdi or Pune International Airport (SAG / PNQ). We can recommend suitable flights and timings on request.

items.question: What is the best time for this pilgrimage?
items.answer: November to February offers the most comfortable weather. The sites are open year-round, with festivals like Ram Navami and Guru Purnima at Shirdi drawing the largest crowds. Nashik is especially significant during the Kumbh Mela years.

items.question: What is Trimbakeshwar?
items.answer: Trimbakeshwar, near Nashik, is one of the twelve Jyotirlingas, the most sacred shrines of Lord Shiva in India. It is the source of the Godavari river and a major pilgrimage site, included in this itinerary.

items.question: Is vegetarian food available throughout?
items.answer: Yes. These pilgrimage towns are largely vegetarian, and Shirdi in particular runs a large prasadalaya. Pure vegetarian food is plentiful and good at hotels and restaurants along the route.
